papers.title: AlphaFold 3
papers.contribution: First unified framework for protein-nucleic acid-small molecule complex prediction; 2024 Nobel Prize.

papers.title: Boltz-2
papers.contribution: First model approaching FEP-level binding affinity at 1000x speed; outperformed all CASP16 affinity participants.

papers.title: RFdiffusion2
papers.contribution: Enzyme design from functional group geometries; scaffolded all 41 active sites in benchmark.

papers.title: ESM3
papers.contribution: 98B multimodal protein LM; generated novel GFP at 58% identity -- simulating 500M years of evolution.

papers.title: Chai-2
papers.contribution: Zero-shot antibody design with 16% hit rate across 52 targets (100x improvement); first GPCR agonist antibodies.

papers.title: Protenix-v1
papers.contribution: First fully open-source model outperforming AF3; Apache 2.0 license; 368M params.

papers.title: The AI drug revolution needs a revolution
papers.contribution: AI drug discovery must incorporate functional human data; hasn't improved ~90% clinical failure rate.

papers.title: ISM001-055 Phase IIa (first AI drug clinical validation)
papers.contribution: First AI-designed drug (target + molecule) completes Phase IIa; dose-dependent FVC improvement in IPF.

papers.title: AF3 in Drug Discovery: Comprehensive Assessment
papers.contribution: AF3 struggles with conformational changes >5A; cannot rank binding affinities; persistent GPCR bias.

papers.title: Critical Assessment of ML models for TDC ADMET
papers.contribution: Only 3/top TDC models pass reproducibility checks; data leakage in several top methods. Deliberately overfitted models could achieve top rankings.

papers.title: CASP16 monomer assessment
papers.contribution: Single-domain fold prediction 'nearly solved'; AF3 improves confidence over AF2.

papers.title: CASP16 complex assessment
papers.contribution: Complex prediction unsolved; >30% of targets intractable; antibody-antigen remains very hard.

papers.title: AI drug clinical trial analysis
papers.contribution: Phase I: 80-90% success (above average); Phase II: ~40% (industry average) -- AI hasn't cracked biology. 8 leading AI drug companies had 31 drugs in human trials by mid-2024.
